name: Galician

ancestors.notes: Galaico-Portuguese: the common ancestor. Galician diverged from Portuguese in the 14th c. after the Portuguese court moved to Lisbon and Galicia came under increasing Castilian political control.
Gallaecian Celtic substrate, strong in northwest Iberia. Possible influences on prosody and nasal vowel development.

Asturleonese/Leonese adstrate: after political separation from Portugal, Galicia remained within the Crown of Castile/León. Castilian/Leonese influence intensified throughout the late medieval and early modern period, contributing to: betacism (v→b), z=/z/ retention, ⟨ll⟩ and ⟨ñ⟩ orthographic conventions (Castilian over Portuguese lh/nh), and loss of some Galaico-Portuguese features.
Suebi superstrate: inherited through Galaico-Portuguese, centered in Gallaecia.
Andalusi Arabic: limited contact in the north, some lexical items through Castilian intermediary.
Occitan: troubadour influence on medieval Galician-Portuguese lyric poetry.
Lusitanian substrate: pre-Celtic IE of western Iberia, inherited through Galaico-Portuguese.

notes: Standard Galician (galego) per the Real Academia Galega (RAG) *Normas ortográficas e morfolóxicas do idioma galego* (2003, revised 2012). Spoken by ~2.4 million people in the Autonomous Community of Galicia (Spain) and diaspora communities. KEY FEATURES vs. Portuguese: (1) SESEO: no /θ/ phoneme — ⟨c⟩ before e/i and ⟨z⟩ = /s/ (not /θ/ as in Castilian, not Portuguese /s̻/). (2) APICO-ALVEOLAR /s/ [s̺] like Castilian/Asturian (not Portuguese predorsal [s̻]). (3) BETACISM: ⟨b⟩ = ⟨v⟩ = /b/ (unlike Portuguese which preserves /v/ ≠ /b/). (4) ⟨ll⟩ = /ʎ/, ⟨ñ⟩ = /ɲ/ (Castilian orthographic conventions, not Portuguese ⟨lh⟩/⟨nh⟩). (5) ⟨ch⟩ = /tʃ/ (standard pronunciation; urban registers increasingly /ʃ/ under Portuguese/Spanish influence). (6) ⟨x⟩ = /ʃ/ — covers many words where Castilian uses ⟨j⟩/⟨ge,gi⟩ (e.g. 'xeneral' [ʃeneˈɾal] vs. Spanish 'general'). (7) NO VOWEL REDUCTION: unstressed /a/ stays [a] (not /ɐ/ as in Portuguese). (8) 7 oral vowel qualities in tonic position: /a, e, ɛ, i, o, ɔ, u/. (9) NASAL VOWELS word-finally (irmán [ɐ̃], corazón [õ]). (10) Preserves diphthongs: ou /ow/ ('cousa'), ei /ej/ ('leite'). KEY FEATURES vs. Asturian: Galician has seseo (no /θ/), no Leonese diphthongization in the same systematic way, descends from Galaico-Portuguese not Asturleonese. Sources: Freixeiro Mato (2006) *Gramática da Lingua Galega*, Regueira (1996), Ferreiro (1997).

sources.author: Carballo Calero, R.
sources.title: Gramática elemental del gallego común (7th ed.)
